description: Structural cross-domain premise burden and top-K router reachability over the 432 held-out cross-domain TEST theorems. Pure distributional pass (no windows, no ranker) isolating the COMBINATORIAL ceiling above any ranker. For each cross-domain goal: n_ooh (out-of-home clustered premises), n_foreign (distinct foreign clusters). Router (TRAIN-only W, e-0011 machinery) routes home+top-K neighbours; complete coverage is POSSIBLE only if EVERY needed foreign cluster is in the home's top-K. router_complete@K = frac of cross-domain goals all of whose foreign clusters are routed -> a HARD ceiling on bridge complete-coverage, independent of B and ranker. Swept K in {1,5,10,20,50}.
motivation: a-0025's decisive bound was that even a PERFECT cross-domain gate buys only complete coverage (+0.048 @B=1000), not recall, and the chain never measured WHY that ceiling sits so low. This isolates the structural cause: how concentrated is cross-domain premise need, and does the static top-K router even REACH the foreign cluster a proof requires.
formal_results: n_test=873, n_cross=432 (frac 0.495). n_ooh: mean 2.78, median 2, frac_eq1 0.463. n_foreign: mean 1.70, median 1, frac_eq1 0.595, frac_le2 0.822. router_complete@K: K1 0.213, K5 0.674, K10 0.861, K20 0.977, K50 0.993. router_any@K: K1 0.444, K5 0.894, K10 0.951. foreign-cluster routed micro@K: K5 0.762, K10 0.908.
results_description: Cross-domain premise need is CONCENTRATED: 59.5% of cross-domain goals need exactly one foreign cluster, 82% need <=2; 46% need a single out-of-home premise. But the chain's standard K=5 router structurally reaches ALL needed foreign clusters for only 67.4% of cross-domain goals -- so ~1/3 are uncompletable at K=5 before any window/ranking loss, exactly bounding a-0025's low oracle complete-coverage gain. Widening the router lifts the ceiling steeply (K10 0.861, K20 0.977) but reintroduces a-0024's distractor dilution.
conclusions: Bridge's complete-coverage payoff is capped by router BREADTH, not window size: at K=5 the ceiling is 0.674 of cross-domain goals. Because need is concentrated (60% single foreign cluster), the right lever is a GOAL-SPECIFIC router (route the 1-2 clusters THIS goal needs) rather than a wider static one that dilutes the within-domain majority.
